subject: Computer Networking
title: DHCP: Automatic Network Setup
description: A 60-second lesson on DHCP: Automatic Network Setup.

hook.text: You plug in a cable or join Wi-Fi. Instantly your device gets an IP address. You didn't configure anything.

buildup.text: DHCP — Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol — assigns IP addresses, gateways, and DNS servers automatically.

discovery.text: Your device broadcasts 'I need an address.' The DHCP server replies with a lease: an IP for a set time.

twist.text: Leases expire. If your device disappears, the address returns to the pool. No manual cleanup needed.

climax.text: Without DHCP, every device would need manual configuration. Large networks would be unmanageable.

punchline.text: DHCP makes joining a network effortless. It just works.

quiz.question: What does DHCP provide to a device joining a network?
options: An IP address, gateway, and DNS configuration automatically
A website's content from a CDN
An encrypted tunnel to a remote server
